questions.prompt: A real-time operating system (RTOS) differs from a general-purpose OS primarily in that it:
choices: B. Guarantees tasks complete within bounded, deterministic time deadlines
C. Runs only on ARM processors
questions.explanation: RTOS: deterministic scheduling, guaranteed response times. Hard real-time: missing deadline = system failure. Soft real-time: missing deadline is tolerable.

questions.prompt: Interrupt latency is defined as the time from:
choices: A. System power-on to first interrupt
B. When an interrupt is asserted to when the ISR begins executing
C. ISR start to ISR completion
D. Task creation to task execution
questions.explanation: Interrupt latency: time from interrupt assertion to first ISR instruction. Includes: context save, interrupt detect, vector fetch. Critical in real-time systems.

questions.prompt: PWM (Pulse Width Modulation) controls analog output power by:
choices: B. Varying the duty cycle (ON time / period) of a digital signal
questions.explanation: PWM duty cycle D = t_on/T. Average output ≈ D × V_supply. Used for motor control, LED dimming, power converters.

questions.prompt: The I²C (Inter-Integrated Circuit) serial protocol uses:
choices: A. 4 wires: MOSI, MISO, SCK, CS
B. 2 wires: SDA (data) and SCL (clock), supports multiple masters and slaves
D. 3 wires with dedicated chip-select per device
questions.explanation: I²C: 2-wire bus (SDA, SCL), open-drain with pull-ups. Multi-master capable, 7-bit addresses (128 devices). Speeds: 100 kHz, 400 kHz, 1 MHz.

questions.prompt: SPI (Serial Peripheral Interface) uses how many wires for full-duplex communication?
questions.explanation: SPI: MOSI (master out slave in), MISO (master in slave out), SCLK (clock), CS (chip select per slave). Full-duplex, faster than I²C (tens of MHz).

questions.prompt: UART (Universal Asynchronous Receiver-Transmitter) communication is:
choices: A. Synchronous, clock shared between devices
B. Asynchronous — no shared clock; uses start/stop bits and agreed baud rate
questions.explanation: UART: asynchronous serial. Start bit (0) + 8 data bits + stop bit (1). Both ends must agree on baud rate. Simple, 2-wire (TX, RX) communication.

questions.prompt: A watchdog timer (WDT) in an embedded system is used to:
choices: A. Measure elapsed time for tasks
B. Automatically reset the system if the software fails to 'pet' it within a specified period
questions.explanation: WDT: if software hangs or crashes (fails to periodically reset the WDT), the WDT times out and resets the MCU. Improves reliability.

questions.prompt: An ADC (Analog-to-Digital Converter) with n-bit resolution and reference voltage V_ref has quantization step size (LSB) of:
questions.explanation: ADC LSB = V_ref / 2^n. For 12-bit ADC with V_ref = 3.3 V: LSB = 3.3/4096 ≈ 0.806 mV.

questions.prompt: The Nyquist criterion for an ADC sampling an analog signal of bandwidth f_max requires sampling rate f_s to be:
questions.explanation: Nyquist: f_s > 2f_max to avoid aliasing. In practice, an anti-aliasing LPF is applied before the ADC.

questions.prompt: Memory-mapped I/O in embedded systems means GPIO registers are accessed by:
choices: B. Reading/writing specific memory addresses assigned to the peripheral
D. A dedicated I/O bus separate from memory
questions.explanation: Memory-mapped I/O: peripheral registers appear at fixed memory addresses. Pointer-based access in C: volatile uint32_t *gpio = (uint32_t*)0x40020000.

questions.prompt: The 'volatile' keyword in embedded C code is used to:
choices: B. Tell the compiler not to optimize accesses to this variable (it can change outside program flow, e.g., hardware registers or ISR-shared variables)
questions.explanation: volatile: compiler must read/write the actual memory location every time, not cache in a register. Essential for hardware registers, ISR-shared globals.

questions.prompt: A task scheduler in an RTOS with preemptive priority scheduling will:
choices: A. Run tasks in round-robin only
B. Immediately switch to a higher-priority task when it becomes ready
C. Wait until the current task finishes before switching
D. Run tasks in order of creation
questions.explanation: Preemptive: higher-priority ready task immediately preempts the running lower-priority task. Cooperative: task must yield voluntarily.

questions.prompt: Priority inversion in an RTOS occurs when:
choices: A. A high-priority task is blocked because a low-priority task holds a needed resource
B. Tasks are scheduled in wrong order
D. Interrupt priority is set incorrectly
questions.explanation: Priority inversion: high-priority task blocked on mutex held by low-priority task, which in turn preempted by medium-priority task. Solved by priority inheritance.

questions.prompt: A DAC (Digital-to-Analog Converter) with 8-bit resolution and 5 V reference can produce how many discrete output levels?
questions.explanation: N-bit DAC: 2^N levels. 8-bit: 2^8 = 256 levels, step size = 5/255 ≈ 19.6 mV.

questions.prompt: Which are common serial communication protocols used in embedded systems? (Select ALL that apply.)
questions.explanation: All four are widely used embedded serial protocols. CAN is common in automotive. I²C/SPI for sensors. UART for debug/host communication.

questions.prompt: A timer configured to generate an interrupt every T_int seconds can create a periodic task by:
choices: B. Triggering an ISR at each timer overflow/compare match to execute time-critical tasks
C. Resetting the system every T_int
questions.explanation: Timer interrupt: timer counts to reload value → interrupt → ISR executes task (e.g., read sensor, update PWM). Fundamental real-time mechanism.

questions.prompt: A 10-bit ADC has a 5 V reference. The quantization step size (LSB) in mV is approximately:
The purpose of an anti-aliasing filter placed before an ADC is to:
choices: B. Remove frequency components above f_s/2 to prevent aliasing
C. Stabilize the ADC reference voltage
D. Convert from differential to single-ended
questions.explanation: Anti-aliasing LPF: attenuates signal above f_s/2 before sampling. Prevents high-frequency components from folding into baseband.

questions.prompt: The main advantage of an FPGA over a microcontroller for real-time signal processing is:
choices: B. Massive parallel hardware execution — many operations truly simultaneous
questions.explanation: FPGA: implements custom digital logic in parallel — multiple operations per clock cycle. MCU: sequential execution. FPGA preferred for high-speed DSP, vision, crypto.

questions.prompt: Flash memory in embedded systems is used for:
choices: B. Non-volatile program storage (firmware/code) and persistent data
questions.explanation: Flash: non-volatile, can be electrically erased/programmed. Used for firmware (code), calibration data, bootloader. Slower than RAM; limited write cycles.

questions.prompt: Debouncing a mechanical switch in software is needed because:
choices: B. Mechanical contacts bounce (make and break multiple times) for a few milliseconds after a press, generating multiple transitions
C. MCU GPIO pins have high capacitance
questions.explanation: Contact bounce: during transition, contacts briefly open/close multiple times (~5-50 ms). Debounce: read stable level, wait (e.g., 20 ms), re-read. Prevents phantom presses.

questions.prompt: A semaphore in an RTOS is used primarily to:
choices: B. Signal between tasks or protect shared resources from simultaneous access
questions.explanation: Semaphore: binary semaphore = mutex (mutual exclusion for shared resources). Counting semaphore = signaling (producer/consumer). Prevents race conditions.

questions.prompt: The power modes of a microcontroller (e.g., sleep, deep sleep, standby) are used to:
choices: A. Increase clock speed during idle
B. Reduce power consumption by halting the CPU and/or peripherals when not needed
D. Switch between internal and external clocks
questions.explanation: Low-power modes: CPU halted, peripherals disabled → current drops from mA to μA range. Critical for battery-powered IoT devices.

questions.prompt: In interrupt-driven firmware, a common best practice is to keep ISRs short because:
choices: A. ISRs run slower than tasks
B. Long ISRs increase interrupt latency and can block higher-priority real-time events
C. ISRs cannot access global variables
questions.explanation: Short ISRs reduce jitter and latency. Typical pattern: ISR captures minimal data and signals a task/queue for heavier processing.

questions.prompt: A periodic task must run every 5 ms. What is its frequency in Hz?
questions.explanation: Frequency f = 1/T = 1/0.005 s = 200 Hz.
